lesson: The attempt was the old worked-exposition countersubject: note-for-note quarters shadowing the subject (rhythmic_homorhythm 1.00) - a harmonization wearing a countersubject's name. The correction applies the species curriculum to the fugue itself: syncopated second species, attacking in the subject's gaps with tones held across its beats (co-attack 0.4), stepwise cells rising to one peak, cadencing on the leading tone. Invertibility survives because downbeats use only unisons, thirds, and sixths - fifths are barred since they invert to fourths. The answer is a cantus; the countersubject is species counterpoint against it, in its own rhythmic stratum.
